ill: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iranun.
illusion: noun. A distortion of sensory perception where real stimuli lead to a false or misleading impression of reality.
ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
insoul: verb. To set a soul in; reflexively, to fix one's strongest affections on.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
lin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Lingala.
loin: noun. The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips.
louis: noun. Any gold or silver coin issued by the French kings from Louis XIII to Louis XVI and bearing their image on the obverse side,
nil: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nila.
nill: verb. To be unwilling; will not (+ infinitive).
nills: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of nill
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nous: noun. The mind or intellect, reason, both rational and emotional.
null: noun. A non-existent or empty value or set of values.
nus: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nuer.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
sill: noun. A breast wall; window breast; horizontal brink which forms the base of a window.
silo: noun. A vertical building, usually cylindrical, used for the production of silage.
soil: noun. A mixture of mineral particles and organic material, used to support plant growth.
sol: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Solos.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
